_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national (autopilot)
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize TX state House batch HD-74..78 (El Paso / South TX border): Eddie Morales (D, HD74), Mary Gonzalez (D, HD75), Suleman Lalani (D, HD76 Fort Bend), Vincent Perez (D, HD77), Joe Moody (D, HD78). State tier (49 cells). TRUE=documented aligned, FALSE=documented contrary, null=no evidence (never inferred from party). Votes verified via TX House roll calls, issue-group scorecards (Texas Right to Life, Texas Alliance for Life, Texas Values Action, ATPE Teach the Vote, The New American Freedom Index), and the members' own statements. Cross-party deviations flagged: Morales (YEA constitutional carry HB1927, YEA drag/book/trans-athlete bills, supports physical border barrier, stayed during 2021 quorum break); Lalani (YEA gold/silver money HB1056, YEA firearm-purchase-privacy HB2837, YEA ERIC withdrawal SB1070, FOR SCR19 Life Monument).

eddie-morales@TX.notes_set: HD-74 Democrat (Eagle Pass attorney; 11-county ~770-mile border district from Maverick to El Paso; in office since 2021). Won the 3/3/2026 Democratic primary; faces R Robert Garza in the 11/3/2026 general. The most conservative House Democrat by multiple measures and one of only ~5 Democrats to back constitutional carry. Documented CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS: voted YEA on HB1927 (2021 constitutional/permitless carry) as one of only five Democrats; voted YEA on the 2023 drag-performance restrictions, the trans-collegiate-athletes bill (SB15), and the HB900 library-content 'book-ban' bill; was one of ~4 Democrats who STAYED in the chamber rather than join the 2021 quorum break; his campaign platform supports a physical border barrier and active border security. Texas Alliance for Life 69% (2025) — unusually high for a Democrat. Contrary votes: NAY on SB14 (2023 minors gender-transition ban); NAY on SB2 (2025 ESA vouchers). YCT career 35; Texans for Fiscal Responsibility F. No documented church affiliation. Several 2021/2025 roll calls (SB1, SB10/SB11, 2025 abortion bills, HB7) could not be individually verified and are left null rather than inferred.

2.note: Voted NAY on SB14 (2023 ban on gender-transition procedures for minors) after meeting a trans teen's family. (Note: he DID cross his party to vote YEA on the 2023 trans-collegiate-athletes bill SB15 — captured at cell 4.)

4.note: Cross-party deviation: voted YEA on the 2023 drag-performance restrictions, the trans-collegiate-athletes bill (SB15), and the HB900 library-content 'book-ban' bill — opposing promotion of LGBTQ identity/content in schools.

0.note: Voted NAY on SB2 (2025 ESA school vouchers) and against tabling the Talarico anti-voucher amendment — opposed school choice.

2.note: Cross-party deviation: voted YEA on HB900 (2023 library-content bill restricting sexually explicit material in school libraries).

0.note: Cross-party deviation: campaign platform supports a 'controlled and secured border' with 'manpower, technology and a physical border'; filed legislation for border-road maintenance framed as border security.
Cross-party deviation: voted YEA on HB1927 (2021 constitutional/permitless carry) — one of only five Democrats to support it.

mary-gonzalez@TX.notes_set: HD-75 Democrat (east El Paso County / Socorro / Clint / Fabens; in office since 2013). Vice Chair of House Appropriations (89th); founding Chair of the Texas House LGBTQ Caucus (since 2019); first openly pansexual elected official in the U.S. Ran in the 3/3/2026 Democratic primary (reported unopposed). Uniformly progressive on this rubric, no cross-party deviation found. HARD record: pro-choice ('Abortion is healthcare'; Reproductive Freedom Leadership Council member; NAY on SB8 Heartbeat and HB1280 trigger ban, 2021), Texas Alliance for Life 45% (2025); actively opposed SB14 (2023 minors gender-transition ban — raised points of order to delay/kill it) and authored the intersex amendment to HB229 (2025); NAY on SB2 vouchers ('absolutely oppose vouchers'); voted by exclusion against HB1927 constitutional carry (not among the named Democratic Yes votes). Note: during the 2021 quorum break she was on a pre-planned vacation in Portugal rather than in D.C. No documented church affiliation.

0.note: Strongly pro-choice; stated 'Abortion is healthcare' and is a member of the Reproductive Freedom Leadership Council — contrary to affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted NAY on SB8 (2021 Heartbeat Act) and HB1280 (2021 trigger ban) — opposed abortion restrictions, no abolition advocacy. Texas Alliance for Life 45% (2025).

0.note: Openly pansexual; founding Chair of the Texas House LGBTQ Caucus — contrary to affirming marriage as one man and one woman.

2.note: Actively opposed SB14 (2023 minors gender-transition ban), raising points of order to delay it; authored the intersex amendment to HB229 (2025) and is in the Democratic nay bloc.

4.note: Founding Chair of the Texas House LGBTQ Caucus — affirmatively advances LGBTQ identity in public policy, contrary to opposing its promotion.

0.note: Voted NAY on SB2 (2025 ESA vouchers); on record 'I absolutely oppose voucher, tax credit, scholarship, education savings account programs in their entirety.'

1.note: Recorded among the votes against SB4 (2023 illegal-entry state crime) — contrary to mandatory-deportation enforcement.

0.note: By exclusion from the named Democratic Yes votes, voted NAY on HB1927 (2021 constitutional carry); El Paso member after the 2019 Walmart massacre.

suleman-lalani@TX.notes_set: HD-76 Democrat (Fort Bend County / Sugar Land; geriatrics/palliative-care physician; Muslim; elected 2022, in office since 2023; with Salman Bhojani one of the first Muslim members of the Texas House). Won the 3/3/2026 Democratic primary; faces R Linda Howell in November. Mostly party-line progressive (Texas Values Action 9%, YCT 16%, Freedom Index 18%) but with several documented CROSS-PARTY DEVIATIONS: voted YEA on HB1056 (2023 gold/silver-backed 'constitutional money'), YEA on HB2837 (2023, banning merchant-code tracking of firearm purchases), YEA on SB1070 (2023 Texas withdrawal from the ERIC voter-data system), YEA on SB17 (2023 DEI ban), and FOR SCR19 (2025 'Texas Life Monument' pro-life resolution). Texas Alliance for Life 56% (2025). Contrary votes: Against SB33 (2025 abortion-funding restriction); HB229 'What is a Woman Act' (2025) and SB1257 Against; SB2 vouchers, SB12 Parents' Bill of Rights, SB13 library content all No; SB10 Ten Commandments, SB11 school prayer, SB965 employee religious speech all Against; SB8 (2025 sheriff-ICE 287g cooperation) Against; HJR98 (Article V convention) and HB796 (Texas Sovereignty Act) No. Per-member 2023 SB14/SB4/HB7 votes not individually confirmed → null.

1.note: Voted Against SB33 (2025 restriction on taxpayer funding for abortion) and Present on SB1233 — no abortion-restriction or abolition advocacy. (Cross-party note: he DID vote FOR SCR19, the 2025 'Texas Life Monument' resolution; Texas Alliance for Life 56%.)

2.note: Voted Against HB229 (2025 'What is a Woman Act', 3rd reading) and Against SB1257 (2025 gender-transition complication coverage) — contrary to affirming immutable biological sex.

0.note: Voted No on SB2 (2025 ESA vouchers); 2020 survey opposed vouchers.

1.note: Voted No on SB12 (2025 Parents' Bill of Rights) — contrary to expanded parental notification/consent.

2.note: Voted No on SB13 (2025 library-content bill restricting sexual material in school libraries).

1.note: Voted Against SB965 (2025 religious-speech/prayer protections for school employees) — contrary to conscience exemptions for educators.

3.note: Voted Against SB10 (Ten Commandments in classrooms, 2nd & 3rd reading) and SB11 (prayer/Bible time in schools), 2025 — contrary to public-square Christian symbols and prayer.

0.note: Cross-party deviation: voted YEA on HB1056 (2023 gold/silver-backed 'constitutional money') — supports sound, decentralized money over fiat/CBDC.
Voted No on HB796 (2023 Texas Sovereignty Act) — contrary to 10th-Amendment nullification/anti-commandeering.

3.note: Voted No on HJR98 (Article V Convention application) and HB796 (Texas Sovereignty Act) — contrary to affirming state sovereignty.

4.note: Cross-party deviation: voted YEA on SB1070 (2023 Texas withdrawal from the multistate ERIC voter-data system) — opposed external capture of state election data.

2.note: Voted Against SB8 (2025 mandatory sheriff–ICE 287(g) cooperation) — contrary to anti-sanctuary cooperation/preemption.

1.note: Cross-party deviation: voted YEA on HB2837 (2023, banning merchant-category-code tracking/registry of firearm purchases) — pro-privacy, anti-registry. (Counterpoint noted: he separately proposed barring registered sex offenders from owning firearms.)

vincent-perez@TX.notes_set: HD-77 Democrat (El Paso; Georgetown grad; former El Paso County Commissioner Pct. 3, 2013-2020; 2016 National County Leader of the Year). Freshman — assumed office Jan 2025 (89th Leg.), succeeding Lina Ortega; running for re-election in the 3/3/2026 Democratic primary. Consistent El Paso Democrat, no cross-party deviation found: endorsed by Planned Parenthood Texas Votes PAC (Texas Alliance for Life 41%, Texas Values Action 5%); anti-voucher (NAY on SB2, vouchers would 'siphon off a billion dollars from our public schools'); supports assault-weapons restrictions ('access to military-style assault weapons... used in almost every horrific mass shooting, including El Paso on August 3'); as County Commissioner built El Paso's first Pretrial Services Office and pushed bail/jail-reduction reforms; joined the Aug 2025 Democratic quorum break over redistricting (fined ~$10,000). Per-bill roll calls otherwise thin (freshman); unverified individual votes left null. No documented church affiliation. NOTE: one secondary source attributed the Planned Parenthood endorsement to his opponent; the El Paso Matters primary attributes it to Perez.

1.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Texas Votes PAC (pro-choice commitment); Texas Alliance for Life 41% — no abortion-restriction or abolition advocacy.

4.note: Accepted the Planned Parenthood Texas Votes PAC endorsement — contrary to refusing abortion-industry backing. (Source-conflict noted in profile.)

0.note: Voted NAY on SB2 (2025 ESA vouchers, all attending Democrats no); stated vouchers would 'siphon off a billion dollars from our public schools.'

1.note: As County Commissioner created El Paso's first Pretrial Services Office and 24/7 magistration to reduce jailing of low-risk/low-income/mentally-ill defendants — a bail-reform posture contrary to opposing cashless-bail/soft-on-crime policy.
Stated residents should not have 'access to military-style assault weapons... designed for the battlefield' — supports an assault-weapons ban, contrary to opposing such bans.

joe-moody@TX.notes_set: HD-78 Democrat (El Paso; lawyer; first elected 2008, lost 2010, reclaimed 2012; chaired Criminal Jurisprudence; Speaker Pro Tempore in the 89th). Member of St. Matthew Catholic Church, El Paso. One of the House impeachment managers against AG Ken Paxton (2023). Filed for re-election in 2026, no declared opponent as of late 2025. The Texas House's leading criminal-justice-reform legislator; uniformly progressive on this rubric (Freedom Index 9% F), no cross-party deviation found. HARD record: NO on HB229 'What is a Woman Act' (2025, among 58 Democratic no votes) and opposed SB14 (2023) authoring a failed amendment to grandfather existing patients; NO on SB2 vouchers, SB12 Parents' Bill of Rights, SB13 library content, and SB10 Ten Commandments (2025); participated in the 2021 quorum break to block SB1 (stripped of Speaker Pro Tem as a result); lead author of marijuana decriminalization (HB218) and a long-standing bail/sentencing-reform leader; opposed HB1927 constitutional carry (2021, authored a failed amendment invoking the El Paso Walmart shooting) and authored 'red-flag' gun-confiscation bills in three consecutive sessions. Texas Alliance for Life 48% likely reflects the bipartisan SB31 maternal-health vote, not a pro-life conversion; left null for lack of a confirmed per-cell vote. Per-member 2021 SB8/HB1280/SB1 and 2023 SB4 votes not individually confirmed → null.

2.note: Voted NO on HB229 (2025 'What is a Woman Act', among 58 Democratic no votes) and opposed SB14 (2023 minors gender-transition ban), authoring a failed amendment to grandfather existing patients.

0.note: Voted NO on SB2 (2025 ESA vouchers) and against tabling the Talarico anti-voucher amendment.

1.note: Voted NO on SB12 (2025 Parents' Bill of Rights).

2.note: Voted NO on SB13 (2025 library-content bill restricting sexual material in school libraries).

3.note: Voted NO on SB10 (2025 Ten Commandments in classrooms) — contrary to public-square Christian displays.

1.note: Participated in the 2021 Democratic quorum break (fled to D.C.) to block SB1, the omnibus election/voter-ID bill — stripped of Speaker Pro Tem as a result.

3.note: The 2021 quorum break opposed SB1, which restricted mail voting, drop boxes, and drive-thru voting — contrary to opposing mass mail-in/harvesting.

1.note: Lead author of marijuana decriminalization (HB218) and a long-standing bail/sentencing-reform leader (chaired Criminal Jurisprudence) — contrary to opposing soft-on-crime/decriminalization policy.

0.note: Opposed HB1927 (2021 constitutional carry), authoring a failed floor amendment invoking the El Paso Walmart shooting.

1.note: Authored 'red-flag' gun-confiscation legislation in three consecutive sessions — contrary to opposing red-flag laws.
